posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
2013 Pierre Gonon St. Joseph - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, St. Joseph (6/27/2015)
Wow, medium body, gorgeous deep violet red fruit, nice electric spice, fine but present tannins, excellent balance. Fabulously delicious, and quite drinkable even at this young age. (92 pts.)

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Nice! Thanks Alan, I have a 6-pack sitting at Chambers waiting for shipping season. I’ve really dug the Iles Feray bottling from this vintage by Gonon. Looking forward to trying the St. Joe.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: The Feray was very tasty as well. Two quite different wines, I liked them both, but the Joe is a real winner this vintage. “Off vintage” strikes again!

posts.name: W E R D N A - S K R O B A C K
posts.cooked: I just must be missing something, as I know this is a board favorite, but I was not impressed at all by the 2012 that I sampled. Just too young?
posts.display_username: W E R D N A - S K R O B A C K

posts.name: Carlos Delpin
posts.cooked: Had the 2011 tonight and the aromatics were as pretty as always but the wine is so tight… My remaining bottles will sleep for a long while.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
A S K R O B A C K:

I just must be missing something, as I know this is a board favorite, but I was not impressed at all by the 2012 that I sampled. Just too young?

I tasted the 2012 at about the same time last year, and actually prefer this 2013 to what is supposedly the stronger 2012 vintage.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: I did not think the '12 showed that great on release, rather grapey for this winery, but I tucked them away without concern.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
A S K R O B A C K:

I just must be missing something, as I know this is a board favorite, but I was not impressed at all by the 2012 that I sampled. Just too young?

I had a beautiful bottle of the 2012 earlier this month. One of the most viscerally drinkable young wines I’ve tasted for a while.

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: The one time I had the 2012 St. Joe I found it overly grapey. I think it is just too young.

posts.name: Daniel McIntosh
posts.cooked: I had the 2012 in October 2014 and yes, it was grapey. And simple.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Out of curiosity I opened a 2012 last night and if it was grapey and simple a year ago it certainly isn’t now. Great stuff.

posts.name: W E R D N A - S K R O B A C K
posts.cooked:
Alan Rath:
A S K R O B A C K:

I just must be missing something, as I know this is a board favorite, but I was not impressed at all by the 2012 that I sampled. Just too young?

I tasted the 2012 at about the same time last year, and actually prefer this 2013 to what is supposedly the stronger 2012 vintage.

I certainly hope so, as the 2012 was “meh” at best.
posts.display_username: W E R D N A - S K R O B A C K

posts.name: Scott Brunson
posts.cooked: Alan, thanks for taking one for the team.

posts.name: RS Beck
posts.cooked: I drank a bottle of the '12 about a year ago, but I drank it over four nights. It started simple and grapey, but definitely improved and revealed different nuances over that time. I’m happy to have some stashed in my cellar. I’ll likely put some '13 in there, too.

posts.name: Leonard Maran
posts.cooked: I have some 2009 & 2010 Pierre Gonon St. Joseph. I opened a bottle of the 2009 and it just made no impression on me, but I’m going to give these wines more time.

posts.name: paul clark
posts.cooked: I opened my first 2013 earlier this week. Drank over 3 nights. This is the most backward vintage I’ve encountered from this domaine. I’ve had bottles from 2001 up through the current release and this year is a stunner. Less opulent and charming than 09&10 the 13 is framed by solid, structured tannins. It’s a year with lively acidity and medium full fruit. Plums and violets are marked by fresh meat, iron and smoke. Gonon had been consistent in the past whereas I felt the 2013 is a sudden departure from that consistency as this wine takes a serious step up in complexity. 09&10 might be stronger vintages providing more pure fruit but 13 offers a more complete wine in this drinker’s view.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Wow, that’s high praise, Paul. And good timing as my 6-pack just came in yesterday. The 2013 Iles Feray was quite good as well.

paul clark:

I’ve had bottles from 2001 up through the current release and this year is a stunner. Less opulent and charming than 09&10 the 13 is framed by solid, structured tannins. It’s a year with lively acidity and medium full fruit. Plums and violets are marked by fresh meat, iron and smoke. Gonon had been consistent in the past whereas I felt the 2013 is a sudden departure from that consistency as this wine takes a serious step up in complexity. 09&10 might be stronger vintages providing more pure fruit but 13 offers a more complete wine in this drinker’s view.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
I stopped by in Mauves a few weeks ago and tasted with Pierre real quick like.
We tried some '14s in tank, the white St Jo was gorgeous, with terrific balance/elegance.
The '14 St Jo as well, fruity, fresh, and savory with very harmonized elements. Lovely.
He told me that in '15, some isolated parcels reached 17% alcohol.!!!
When blended in with everything else, the alcohol will certainly be more moderate, but that the overall blend will definitely be 14%+…
Fwiw.

posts.name: Markus S
posts.cooked:
Robert Panzer:

… the overall blend will definitely be 14%+…
Fwiw.

Shoot, now it can’t be an AFWE wine anymore!
